behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a local module boundary: CSV reader row-index handoff + schema validator + DLQ writer metadata. A unit test of only the validator misses row-number propagation bugs. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no AI/ML output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM stages. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text does not require real S3/real queue transport, only correct rejection and row-number recording.
behaviors.test_description: Run the pipeline in-process with a small CSV fixture and in-memory/fake DLQ sink. Assert rows missing required fields are absent from normalized output and present in DLQ entries with exact original CSV row numbers.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: This is transformation/parsing logic in one boundary; no cross-module invariant is needed to expose the core failure. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior text is specifically about conversion correctness, not handoff/persistence/retry semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests for the date-normalization function: ISO 8601, US, and European inputs map to the same canonical output; invalid/ambiguous formats fail as defined.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: This depends on real runtime characteristics (wall-clock timeout, process memory behavior, GC/backpressure), which in-process fakes cannot faithfully reproduce. MOCK-HIDE applies: mocked iterators/storage can pass while real process OOMs or times out.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the pipeline as a real process against a generated 500k-row CSV and real runtime limits; assert completion before configured timeout and peak RSS stays below threshold (no OOM/kill).

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets real HTTP/service-failure semantics at an external boundary. A pure mock of the S3 client can hide misclassification of real 503 responses or backoff timing behavior. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because behavior explicitly references S3 transient 503 handling, which is an external API failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against an HTTP-backed S3 test endpoint (or LocalStack) configured to return 503 for initial attempts then success. Assert retry count/backoff growth and that batch eventually succeeds instead of hard-failing on first 503.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes persistence + restart semantics across process lifecycle; requires real DB write behavior and restart recovery. In-process mocks can miss transaction/idempotency/checkpoint interactions that produce duplicates after crash/restart.
behaviors.test_description: Start processing a batch against a real PostgreSQL instance, force-stop mid-batch, restart pipeline, then assert final table row set has no duplicates (by business key) and row count equals expected unique inputs.
